items.quote: our CFO has repeatedly asked why is our invoice process per AP team member so low
items.source: Avoma call 2025-05-28, Jeannine Jenkins (reporting Kelly Baker's view secondhand)

items.quote: 10/13/25 RH Invite Chamika to PLaN- determine if initiative is stalled, postponed or cancelled.
would you be able to, reschedule another call and we could do, maybe you could do a live demo with us?

metrics.narrative: On the 28 May 2025 call ('Zycus Eversana connect'), Jeannine Jenkins and Chamika McLean quantified the pain in detail: only about 5% of invoices auto-match PO to invoice versus an 80% match rate promised when their current AP tool (Xflow, on Microsoft Dynamics 365 F&O) was implemented roughly two years earlier; invoices needing insufficient-fund rework are touched '4 or 5 times'; and the AP team fields '30 to 45, 50 requests a day' in a dedicated vendor-inquiry inbox. Jeannine also reported, secondhand, that 'our CFO has repeatedly asked why is our invoice process per AP team member so low,' tying the inefficiency directly to CFO-level scrutiny. No formal ROI or dollar-value business case has been built since, and this data point is now roughly 13 months old.

champion.narrative: Chamika McLean drove the relationship from the start: she took the original discovery call, brought Jeannine Jenkins into the 28 May 2025 session, asked to receive the recording so she could 'converse with additional individuals in her organization,' and directly asked the Zycus rep, 'would you be able to reschedule another call and we could do... a live demo with us?' That is real, buyer-initiated advocacy. But she has not replied to any outreach since a confirmed email reply on 5 Jun 2025 (roughly 13 months ago as of this sweep), the promised demo she requested was never delivered, and her access to the named Economic Buyer (Kelly Baker) has never been demonstrated.

competition.narrative: The named incumbent is Xflow (Microsoft D365 F&O AP module) - incumbent tool, live inside Eversana's ERP for 'a little over two years' as of 28 May 2025, delivering only a 5% automatic PO-to-invoice match against an expected 80%. No competing vendor other than this incumbent has been named in any call or field. The live threat is not a rival vendor but Status quo (internal AP process inertia / initiative shelved): with no confirmed buyer reply in roughly 13 months and the rep's own plan being to check whether the initiative is 'stalled, postponed or cancelled,' the real risk is that the evaluation has quietly died internally.

identify_pain.narrative: Deep, first-hand pain was surfaced on 28 May 2025: manual 3-way-match workarounds performed by their Chief Accounting Officer 'hours ... every week,' a real example of a $50,000 PWC invoice the system cannot auto-allocate across departments, invoices erroring out only at final GL posting due to invisible PO fund shortfalls, and unapplied cash from vendors unable to auto-apply remittances. This came directly from Chamika McLean and Jeannine Jenkins, not rep inference.

sources: Absence across all three read touchpoints and the current Next Step
paper_process.narrative: No commercial, pricing, or contracting conversation has occurred at any point across the record read; the deal has never left the discovery/demo-scheduling phase.

sources: Contact Role: Kelly Baker, CFO, Decision Maker
economic_buyer.narrative: Kelly Baker (CFO) is named as the Decision Maker in the Salesforce contact role, but she has never appeared on any of the three calls read (12 Mar 2025, 18 Mar 2025, 28 May 2025) and has never replied to outreach. Her stance is known only second-hand via Jeannine Jenkins's comment on 28 May 2025 that the CFO 'has repeatedly asked why is our invoice process per AP team member so low' - indicating awareness of the pain, but no direct Zycus engagement, no budget conversation, and no scheduled executive session on record.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process, approval chain, or evaluation timeline has been discussed on any call read. The rep's own Next Step ('determine if initiative is stalled, postponed or cancelled,' dated 13 Oct 2025) shows Zycus does not currently know whether Eversana's evaluation is still active, let alone who signs off or on what cadence.

decision_criteria.narrative: On 28 May 2025 the buyer team named concrete requirements: a system reaching at least '80% of our wish list' for automatic PO-to-invoice matching (versus their current 5%), support for splitting allocations across departments on blanket/flip POs, visibility into PO fund availability before invoice approval (rather than erroring out at final GL posting), automated capture of cost-per-invoice and cycle-time KPIs, and low-lift integration with their Microsoft Dynamics 365 F&O ERP (the Zycus rep estimated 'maybe 20% of the effort' for IT). These are real, buyer-articulated criteria, but they have never been formalized into a BRD/ShoeFit exercise or validated against a demo.

items.name: Sequence call attempt to Matt Goyer
items.summary: Automated step 10 of the Emerging Enterprise outbound cadence (via Nooks dialer) placed a call to Matt Goyer; no notes or connect outcome recorded, so it's unclear if contact was made — still awaiting a live response.

day_summary.overall: A single dialer task fired today as part of the Emerging Enterprise outbound sequence, with a call attempt to Matt Goyer at Eversana marking step 10 of that cadence. No outcome, notes, or callback details were logged, so this reads as a routine automated touchpoint rather than a substantive conversation — the deal remains stalled at the prospecting/outreach stage, waiting on Matt to actually engage.

cro_panel.intro: This is an early-stage, well-articulated-pain deal that has gone cooling for roughly 13 months; strong discovery-level pain and a real, buyer-initiated champion ask for a demo exist from 28 May 2025, but the economic buyer has never engaged directly, no demo/pricing/contracting conversation has followed, and the imminent 22 Jul 2026 close date has no supporting recent evidence

forecast_read.reason: Pipeline is the right conservative category given the evidence, but the 22 Jul 2026 close date itself is not defensible: the deal has had no confirmed buyer reply since a single email on 5 Jun 2025 (~13 months ago), no demo has been delivered, and the rep's own 13 Oct 2025 Next Step is simply to find out whether the initiative is 'stalled, postponed or cancelled.' The close date should be pushed well beyond 22 Jul 2026 once (if) re-engagement is confirmed.

critical_signals.text: The real threat here isn't a rival vendor, it's inertia: no confirmed buyer reply has come back in roughly 13 months, and the rep's own current plan is simply to find out whether the initiative has been shelved.

In the one deep-discovery call held (28 May 2025), the buyer team laid out a detailed, quantified automation business case and directly asked for a live demo, but that demo was never delivered and no follow-up call has occurred since.

The named Decision Maker, CFO Kelly Baker, has never joined a call or engaged directly; everything known about her view comes second-hand through a colleague quoting her frustration with AP inefficiency.
No pricing, proposal, or contracting conversation has ever taken place; the deal has not moved past initial discovery in over a year.

north_star_verdict.headline: The Eversana AP-automation deal has been effectively dormant for over a year: the last genuine two-way buyer contact was Chamika McLean's 5 Jun 2025 email, and 13 months of steady rep outreach (calls, emails) has drawn no reply since. The 22 Jul 2026 close date (15 days out as of this sweep) is not credible against a deal still sitting in Qualified stage with no demo, pricing, or contracting conversation ever begun.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pull out of the current quarter / push close date materially; no realistic path to close by 22 Jul 2026 given the deal remains in Qualified stage with no live buyer engagement, demo, or pricing conversation on record.

flags: Re-open the thread with Chamika McLean directly — the 5%-match Xflow pain and CFO-level frustration she described on 28 May 2025 remain unresolved and Zycus has a strong technical fit; personal re-engagement is the highest-leverage move available.

Multi-thread beyond Chamika: bring in Matt Goyer (VP Procurement) or Justin Stenz (SVP Finance) as a second point of contact, since the deal currently rests on one AP-side champion who has gone quiet for over a year.
Convert the 28 May 2025 pain data (5% vs 80% match rate, 4-5 touches per invoice, CFO frustration) into a written one-page business case now, before another sweep passes with zero fresh evidence.

Treat the 13-month silence as a re-engagement problem, not a nurture problem: escalate past Chamika McLean to Jeannine Jenkins or Justin Stenz with a fresh, dated ROI hook tied to their own stated 80% match-rate target.

Single-threaded relationship: Chamika McLean is the only contact who has ever replied to Zycus; build a second thread via Jeannine Jenkins, who was looped in on 28 May 2025 but has not been engaged since.
